source_reviews.notes: The live IRS announcement directly confirms eight 2026 private-sector Free File partners, guided preparation and filing, an AGI ceiling of $89,000, and partner-specific eligibility. It materially supports free/DIY price pressure and simple-return self-service, while not constituting a NAICS adoption census as the packet discloses.

The live Form 10-K directly documents assisted and DIY channels, AI Tax Assist for paid DIY online returns, accuracy guarantees, refund-transfer and advance products, 11.3 million assisted returns, 3.8 million paid DIY online returns, and an approximately 30% U.S. franchise royalty. It supports provider workflow, pricing-product, brand-network, and competitive-direction claims; the packet discloses that a major chain is not representative of every independent.

The live May 12, 2026 GAO decision confirms Treasury suspended IRS Direct File in fall 2025 and that the service would be unavailable going forward. It supports the narrative correction but is not referenced by any finalized score input.

publication_caveats: inputs.ai_replaceable_share: Actual 541213 workforce is seasonal preparers/intake, unlike 4-digit CPA-heavy role mix.
inputs.pi_dist: Free File eligibility and offerings vary; H&R Block is a major-chain proxy rather than all independents.
inputs.pi_moat: Franchise and independent client-file ownership differs.
inputs.t50_years: Not a code-level adoption census; complex work is less automated.
inputs.current_adoption_pct: No reopened NAICS adoption survey.
inputs.owners_60plus_pct.succession_shortage_documented: Absence of evidence is not evidence of no issue.
inputs.active_consolidators: Networks need not acquire independent 541213 firms.
inputs.buy_mult: No reopened code-specific sale comparables; SDE and EBITDA differ.
inputs.exit_mult: No reopened code-specific sale comparables.
inputs.pricing_structure: H&R Block does not represent every tax preparer.
summary: All deterministic mechanics and frozen digests pass, and all three registered sources are live. IRS Free File and H&R Block's filed channel, AI-assistance, franchise, and refund-product evidence support the useful conclusion that simple consumer preparation is highly automatable but offers weak capture and consolidation economics; GAO confirms Direct File is suspended. Publication requires the authored caveats because adoption above 50%, role weights, independent-firm economics, owner demographics, consolidator count, and multiples are disclosed estimates or broad proxies rather than code-level observations. No inaccessible, contradicted, or materially misrepresented score-driving evidence warrants rejection.
